detaileddescription.para: Hines, junctions. Flattened array containing the off-diagonal elements of the Hines matrix

Hines, series. Flattened array containing the tridiagonal of the approximately tridiagonal Hines matrix, stacked against the column vector "b" that appears on the RHS of the equation that we're trying to solve: Ax=b.

Contains a list of all children of a given compt. Also contains the parent itself. i.e., for each compartment that has more than one child, coupled_ stores a vector containing the children of the compartment and the compartment itself. coupled_ is therefore a vector of such vectors.

memberdef.definition: map< unsigned int, unsigned int > HinesMatrix::groupNumber_
memberdef.name: groupNumber_

detaileddescription.para: Tells you the index of a compartment's group within coupled_, given the compartment's Hines index.
Contains iterators into HJ_ demarcating where a child's neighbours begin. Used for iterating through HJ_ along with junction_.

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned int row, unsigned int col) const
memberdef.name: getA

detaileddescription.para: This function creates junction structs to be stored in junction_. It does so by first looking through all compartments and finding those which have more than zero or one child. The zero-one child compts are left alone. coupled_ is populated with the rest of the compartments. Each element of coupled_ is sorted (they are all unsigned ints) and coupled_ itself is sorted by the first element in each element (groupCompare does this comparison in HinesMatrix.cpp). Note: the children themselves are unsigned ints that store the Hines index of the corresponding child compartment. So essentially, at each branch, a JunctionStruct is created for each child, which contains the hines index of that child and its rank, which is group-size() - childIndex - 1.
Populates HS_ and HJ_. All of the electrical circuit analysis goes into this one single function (and updateMatrix, of course).
Makes operands in order to make forward
